free_text: Reactions of V-51 and Al-27 with 7-120 MeV Alpha-
Particles - Equilibrium and Non-Equilibrium Statistical
Analyses
.Projectile Energies Within
Target Stacks were Calculated According to C.Williamson
.Incident Beam Energy 42.0+-0.1 MeV,
Beam Energy on the Last of 15 Target Units 7+-2 MeV.
.Incident Beam Energy 120.0+-0.5 MeV,
Beam Energy on the Last of 55 Target Units 61+-4 MeV.
.Incident Beam Energy 85.7+-0.5 MeV,
Beam Energy on the Last of 40 Target Units 46+-2 MeV.
The Cross Sections were Calculated from the Sum of the
Activities of the Product Nuclide in the Target- And
The Catcher Foils.
.There are So Many Points Given in the
Figures That It is Difficult to Recognize All of Them.
Therefore, Some of the Experimental Values May be
Missing in the Table. Comment by Kachapag.
Mzla
Some Corrections have Been Included by
CAJAD
Lower Case. Sample and Err-Analys are
Corrected. Some Reaction Lines are Corrected.
Last checking has been done.
Foils of Natural Vanadium, Thickness Approximately
12.5 Micrometer, Purity 99.9 %, Each Followed by One
Aluminium Catcher Foil of the Same Thickness and
Additional Aluminium Beam-Energy Degrader Foils Placed
Between these Units For the Higher-Energy Bombardments.
The Errors Given Include Uncertainties in
Target Thickness, Counting Statistics, Detector
Efficiencies, Beam Current,Decay Scheme Data and
Photopeak Integration.
The Estimated Ranges of Error are 20 to 30 %. Some
Characteristic Values are Given in the Figures. See
Data Section.
